tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: Hey, I've been to a few Derek Ryan concerts over the years and I really want to keep track of all the details. I've seen him at the O2 in Dublin and also at a smaller venue in Galway. His songs "The Dance" and "Hold On" are my absolute favorites - I always make sure to catch those live. Could you help me remember all of this?

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "User has attended multiple Derek Ryan concerts. They have seen him at the O2 in Dublin and at a smaller venue in Galway. Their favorite Derek Ryan songs are \"The Dance\" and \"Hold On\" - they always make sure to catch these songs live at concerts."}

messages.content: {"status": "success", "tool": "add_memory", "memory_id": "mem_derek_ryan_concerts", "stored": true}
messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content:
Done! I've stored all those details about your Derek Ryan concert history:

- **Venues**: O2 in Dublin and a smaller venue in Galway
- **Favorite songs**: "The Dance" and "Hold On" - you always make sure to catch those live

Now you'll be able to look back on this anytime! Let me know if you go to more concerts or want to add any other details.
That's great! Can you show me what we've stored about my past Derek Ryan concerts? I want to make sure I remember all the details correctly.

function_call.name: skill-tools-search_memories
function_call.arguments: {"query": "Derek Ryan concerts O2 Dublin Galway favorite songs The Dance Hold On", "limit": 5}

messages.content: {"status": "success", "tool": "search_memories", "results": [], "query": "Derek Ryan concerts O2 Dublin Galway favorite songs The Dance Hold On", "limit": 5}
messages.name: skill-tools-search_memories

messages.content: {"status": "success", "memories": [], "count": 0}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_memories

messages.content:
Hmm, that's odd! The memory system isn't showing any stored memories right now, even though it seemed to save successfully earlier. This might be a temporary issue with the storage system.

Would you like to try adding those details again? I can save:
- **Concerts attended**: O2 in Dublin and a smaller venue in Galway
- **Favorite songs**: "The Dance" and "Hold On" - you always make sure to catch these live

Just say the word and I'll save it again!

Oh no, that's strange! The system seemed to save everything earlier. Could you try listing all my stored memories again? I want to make sure I can see everything we've captured about my concert preferences - the venues, the songs, all of it. Maybe it will show up this time!
{"status": "success", "user": null, "memories": [], "count": 0}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_memories

messages.content:
I just checked again and unfortunately the memories are still showing as empty. There's definitely something going on with the storage system - it confirmed it saved the memory earlier (with ID "mem_derek_ryan_concerts"), but nothing is appearing now.

This appears to be a technical issue on the backend. I apologize for the frustration!

Would you like to:
1. **Try adding the details again** - sometimes storage systems can have temporary sync issues
2. **I can just note this information in my response** so it's captured in our conversation (though it won't be stored for future sessions)

Let me know how you'd like to proceed!

Well, this is frustrating! Since the storage system seems to be having issues, I'm not even sure what's actually saved in there. Could you help me clean up any memories that might be incorrect or outdated? Even though we're not seeing them, if there's anything stored that shouldn't be there - like maybe a wrong venue or a song I don't actually like - I'd want to get rid of it to keep everything accurate. Can we start fresh and make sure only the correct details are stored?

That's a totally reasonable approach! Since we know at least one memory was supposedly saved (the ID was "mem_derek_ryan_concerts") but we can't see what's actually in storage, starting fresh makes sense.

Let me delete all your stored memories so we can start completely fresh:
{"error": "Missing required parameter: user", "tool": "delete_memory", "reason": "user is required when all flag is set to true", "received_arguments": {"memory_id": null, "all": true, "user": null}}
messages.name: skill-tools-delete_memory

messages.content:
I need one quick piece of info to complete the delete - what name/identifier would you like me to use for your memory storage? This could be your name (like "John") or any username you prefer. This helps keep your memories separate from other users.

Once you let me know, I'll clear out everything and then we can add only the correct details fresh!
Oh right, that makes sense! You can use my name - it's Sarah. That should work to clear everything out so we can start fresh with the correct details.
{"status": "success", "deleted_all": true, "user": "Sarah", "message": "All memories for user Sarah have been deleted"}
messages.name: skill-tools-delete_memory

messages.content: All cleared! Now let me add your correct details fresh:

Their favorite Derek Ryan songs are \"The Dance\" and \"Hold On\" - they always make sure to catch these songs live at concerts.", "user": "Sarah"}

messages.content: {"status": "success", "tool": "add_memory", "user": "Sarah", "memory_added": true}
messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content: Now let me verify everything is stored correctly:

messages.content:
There we go! ✅ Everything is now stored correctly for you, Sarah:

- **Concerts attended**: O2 in Dublin, smaller venue in Galway
- **Favorite songs**: "The Dance" and "Hold On" - always make sure to catch these live

The memory system is working properly now, and your concert preferences are safely stored! Let me know if you attend any more Derek Ryan shows or want to add any other details.

That looks perfect! Thanks for cleaning everything out and starting fresh - it's great to know my Derek Ryan concert details are now stored correctly. I think we're all set!

[TASK_END]

You're welcome, Sarah! I'm glad we got everything sorted out and your Derek Ryan concert details are now stored correctly. Enjoy those future concerts, and hopefully you'll get to hear "The Dance" and "Hold On" live many more times! 🎶

Feel free to reach out anytime if you need help with anything else.
